tldr: Move transactional email off Amazon SES to Plivo if you want a single multi-channel API for SMS, voice, and (where supported) email.
why_switch: Consolidate SMS, voice, and messaging behind a single vendor instead of stitching SES + a separate SMS provider
Plivo has predictable per-message pricing and a public pricing page
Strong SDKs and developer experience reduce integration time
Less coupling to the AWS account/IAM model if you want to leave AWS
what_to_export: List of verified sending domains and addresses
DKIM/SPF/DMARC DNS records currently configured for SES
Configuration sets and event destinations (CloudWatch, SNS, Kinesis Firehose)
IAM users / API credentials referenced by your senders
Sending identities and any custom MAIL FROM domains
Webhook / event handlers for bounces and complaints
Historical send logs needed for compliance
export_method: From the AWS console or CLI, list verified identities, configuration sets, templates, and the account-level suppression list. Save DNS records (DKIM CNAMEs, SPF, DMARC, custom MAIL FROM) and any IAM policies tied to SES. Pull bounce/complaint history from your CloudWatch / SNS / S3 destination, since SES itself does not retain a long-term log. Consult the official Amazon SES documentation for the exact CLI commands and current export options.
import_method: Create a Plivo account, then re-create your sending identities and re-publish the corresponding DNS records on your domain. Re-upload templates and import your suppression list using Plivo's official tooling (consult Plivo's docs for the current import path). Wire up webhooks for delivery, bounce, and unsubscribe events. Run a low-volume warm-up before cutting full traffic over.

concept_mapping.note: Re-verify on Plivo; DKIM keys are provider-specific
Point Plivo events at your existing handlers, adapt payloads

gotchas: Reputation does not transfer between providers — warm up new sending IPs/domains gradually
DKIM keys must be re-issued on Plivo and re-published in DNS; keep SES records live during dual-send
Webhook payload schemas differ; bounce/complaint handling code will need adaptation
Verify Plivo's current product coverage for email vs SMS/voice in your region before cutover
Account-level sending limits and dedicated IPs need to be re-requested
Run SES and Plivo in parallel during cutover to avoid dropping mail

email.subject: Migrating from Amazon SES to Plivo
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to move transactional messaging off Amazon SES onto Plivo so we can consolidate SMS, voice, and email under one API and pricing page.

Planned steps: export verified identities, DNS (DKIM/SPF/DMARC), templates, and the suppression list from SES; re-create them on Plivo; warm up sending; then dual-send before fully cutting over. Webhook handlers will need small changes to match Plivo's event schema.

Estimated effort: 1-3 days of engineering plus warm-up time. I'll share a runbook and rollback plan before we start.

Thanks
